name: Optilogic Supply Chain Design API
what_it_does: Optilogic Cosmic Frog REST API for cloud-native supply chain network design and optimization. Enables AI agents to manage supply chain network model building and facility location optimization for distribution network planning, handle transportation cost optimization and lane analysis for logistics network design, access scenario analysis and sensitivity testing for supply chain resilience planning, retrieve cost-to-serve and total delivered cost analysis for supply chain economics, manage demand variability and stochastic optimization for robust network design, handle sustainability and carbon footprint analysis for ESG supply chain planning, access supply chain risk assessment and disruption modeling for resilience, retrieve prescriptive analytics and optimization results for supply chain decision support, manage multi-echelon inventory positioning across network for inventory optimization, and integrate Optilogic with ERP, TMS, and data warehouse platforms for supply chain data.
use_cases: Designing supply chain networks from strategy agents
Optimizing transportation lanes from logistics agents
Modeling disruption scenarios from resilience agents
Analyzing carbon footprint from sustainability agents
not_for: Day-to-day supply chain execution without strategic design
Small businesses without supply chain network complexity
Real-time operational decisions requiring sub-second response
best_when: A manufacturer, retailer, or logistics company wants AI agents to automate supply chain network modeling, facility location optimization, risk scenario analysis, and sustainability planning using Cosmic Frog.
avoid_when: STRATEGIC DECISION GOVERNANCE: Supply chain network design recommendations (opening/closing facilities, changing distribution strategy) have multi-year financial implications; automated optimization outputs must not trigger execution without executive governance and board approval. Model accuracy dependence — supply chain network optimization quality is entirely dependent on input data accuracy (costs, demands, capacities, distances); automated optimization with poor data creates misleading recommendations that drive costly network changes. Early-stage vendor risk — Optilogic was founded in 2019 and is a relatively young company; evaluate API stability, customer support, and long-term viability before mission-critical integration.

auth.notes: Optilogic uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Python SDK available. Documentation at docs.optilogic.com. Ann Arbor, Michigan HQ. Founded 2019 by former LLamasoft executives (Don Hicks, team). Private (early-stage startup). Cloud-native supply chain design built on Cosmic Frog platform. Democratizing supply chain optimization with cloud-based tools. Competes with LLamasoft/Coupa and River Logic for network design.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available; limited model size and runs
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly and annual subscription; per-user and per-model pricing
pricing.notes: Ann Arbor, Michigan. Founded 2019. Early-stage startup by ex-LLamasoft team. Cloud-native supply chain design. Free tier available. Competes with LLamasoft and River Logic.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; optimization runs are async jobs

known_agent_gotchas: STRATEGIC GOVERNANCE: Supply chain network design outputs affecting facility and supplier decisions require executive review before execution; automated optimization must not trigger real-world changes without governance approval
Async optimization execution — Cosmic Frog optimization runs are asynchronous; complex models take minutes to hours; implement job status polling, not synchronous calls, for production workflows
Early-stage startup API stability — Optilogic founded 2019; API is actively evolving; implement version pinning and change monitoring for production integrations
Python SDK dependency — Optilogic Python SDK simplifies model building but introduces SDK version dependency; pin SDK version and test SDK updates before production deployment
No webhooks — optimization job completion requires polling; no native push notification for model run results

Input data validation — Cosmic Frog optimization is extremely sensitive to data quality; implement data validation (non-negative costs, valid facility capacities, complete demand data) before submitting optimization jobs
agent_readiness.security_notes: Supply chain design. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Supply chain network and cost data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Supply chain design. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Network and logistics data.
